rawText: On 10 July 2026 Nutraceutical Business Review reported Elysium Health's study of its NAD+ product Basis, published in Frontiers in Aging: an open-label pilot of 40 healthy women aged 35 and above, 32 of whom self-reported symptoms associated with the menopausal transition and eight of whom had no or minimal symptoms, reporting reductions of more than 50% in hot flushes, bloating and poor sleep after seven days of supplementation, plus a significant increase in the ratio of estradiol (E2) to estrone (E1) and the characterisation of a previously unreported NAD+ metabolite. The design has no placebo or control arm, the outcomes are self-reported, and the readout is at seven days; co-author Dr Ryan Dellinger is Elysium's VP of Scientific Affairs and co-author Dr Marie Migaud is an Elysium research collaborator who said 'This study stemmed from my own experience with Basis helping with menopausal symptoms'. The authors called the results 'promising' and said they lay groundwork for larger studies; Elysium said it intends to conduct a larger randomised, placebo-controlled trial. · ANGLES: (1) What 'open-label' means when the outcome is a self-reported symptom — no placebo arm, and menopause symptoms are strongly placebo-responsive. (2) The seven-day window — why a one-week readout on hot flushes is a design question, not a result. (3) The company's own concession that a larger placebo-controlled trial is planned, read as the honest caption to the headline. · APP CONNECTION: check evidence fit — paste the claim and the app checks study design against the strength of the claim: is there a control arm, how many people, how long. · SECONDARY: https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/aging/articles/10.3389/fragi.2026.1773667/full (Frontiers in Aging, doi 10.3389/fragi.2026.1773667, grade A — linked from the NBR article but NOT independently fetched; verify every figure against the paper before publishing) · SOURCE GRADE: B (trade journalism, fetched and date-verified 'Published: 10-Jul-2026'), resting on a grade A paper. · RISK NOTE: report the design, not motive. Elysium disclosed the author affiliations and announced its own follow-up trial.
